info.name: LCC User Journey Tests (with Case Registration)
info.description:
Complete LCC API testing collection with AUTOMATIC authentication and Case Registration.

## Quick Start

**Just set 4 variables and run!**

| Variable | Your Value |
|----------|------------|
| `azureUsername` | Your Azure AD email |
| `azurePassword` | Your Azure AD password |
| `cmsUsername` | Your CMS username (e.g., Name.CIN3) |
| `cmsPassword` | Your CMS password |
| `egressWorkspaceName` | Workspace name (sets defendant surname) |

## Sections

1. **Authentication** - Azure AD & CMS auth (auto-handled)
2. **Case Registration** - Register cases with defendants matching workspace name
3. **Case Management** - Search and manage cases  
4. **Egress Integration** - List Egress files via LCC API
5. **NetApp Integration** - List NetApp files
6. **File Transfer** - Egress <-> NetApp transfers (includes Register Case)
7. **System** - Health checks
8. **Egress Direct Upload** - 100MB automated file upload

## Egress to NetApp Workflow

1. Run **5.0 Register Case** - Creates case with defendant = workspace name
2. Run **5.1 List Egress Files** - Gets files from workspace
3. Run **5.2 Validate Transfer** - Validates transfer
4. Run **5.3 Initiate Transfer** - Starts the transfer
5. Run **5.4 Get Transfer Status** - Monitor progress

## Pre-configured (DEV Environment)

- `baseUrl` - LCC API
- `caseApiBaseUrl` - Case Management API (fa-cm-api-dev)
- `ddeiBaseUrl` - DDEI API
- `egressBaseUrl` - Egress API

item.name: 0. E2E Setup
item.description: Shared setup chain run once per journey via Newman --folder. Auth, register case (or default-mode skip + caseId from defaults), tokens, search, Egress + NetApp connections, list workspace files. The last item setNextRequests(null) so Newman terminates cleanly when this folder is run in isolation. Each E2E journey folder is then run as a separate Newman pass using --environment pointed at this folder's --export-environment output.

exec: // Stamp a fresh run id once per Setup pass. The uniquify blocks in the
// journey folders consume it to build per-run destination sub-folders so
// re-importing the generated env into Postman Runner and pressing Run a
// second time writes to a fresh sub-folder and does not FileExists-collide
// on the prior pass. {{$guid}} is freshly resolved by replaceIn each call,
// so each Setup pass overwrites any previous value persisted in the env.
// Default mode: skip the register-case Azure token call entirely.
// This request uses {{registerCaseClientId}}, which is only required
// when -RegisterCase is passed. Without this guard the request still
// fires against Azure with an empty/invalid client_id and 400s before
// setNextRequest must precede skipRequest - skipRequest aborts
// the rest of the pre-request script, so anything after it never
// runs and the runner would advance to the natural next item
// (1a. Get CMS Auth for Register Case) instead.
// If registerCase flag is not set, skip register case flow and use existing case
console.log('[SKIP] registerCase not set - using existing caseId: ' + pm.variables.get('defaultCaseId') + ', urn: ' + pm.variables.get('defaultCaseUrn'));
pm.execution.setNextRequest('1a. Get CMS Auth for Register Case');

item.name: 1a. Get CMS Auth for Register Case

exec: pm.test('Response has CMS auth structure', function() {
console.log('[OK] CMS Auth for Register Case obtained');
console.error('Failed to get CMS auth for register case');
// Skip unless registerCase is explicitly 'true' at the request level so that running this
// request from any entry point (single-folder Runner pass, manual Send,
// missing/blank variable, or a folder whose upstream skip guard never fired) does not create a
// new case in CMS. When caseId is already set, reuse it. When skipping
// and defaultCaseId is populated, copy it into the
// active caseId / caseUrn so subsequent requests work, then skip.
console.log('[register-case-guard] registerCase!=true and no defaultCaseId; skipping silently');

request.description:
Verify Egress upload info is set.

## How to Use

1. Run PowerShell script:
   ```
   .\Upload-EgressLargeFile.ps1 -SizeGB 1
   ```

2. Copy output to Postman environment:
   - `egressUploadFileName`
   - `egressWorkspaceId`
   - `egressUploadFolderPath`

3. Run this request to verify

item.description:
Egress upload is done via PowerShell script.

## Run Upload
```powershell
Set-ExecutionPolicy -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -Scope Process
.\Upload-EgressLargeFile.ps1 -SizeGB 1
```

## Variables Set by Script
- `egressUploadFileName` - Uploaded filename
- `egressWorkspaceId` - (from collection variables)
- `egressUploadFolderPath` - Automation API/

item.description:
End-to-end test: Copy file from Egress to NetApp with activity log verification.

## Flow (9 steps)
1. Get Azure AD Token
2. Get CMS Auth
3. Search Case
4. List Egress Files (gets latest by timestamp)
5. Validate Transfer
6. Initiate Transfer
7. Poll Status (loops until complete)
8. Verify Activity Log
9. Download Activity Log

## Response Validation
Each request validates:
- HTTP 200 status
- Required response fields
- Data structure

item.description:
End-to-end test: Copy file from Egress to NetApp with activity log verification.

## Flow (9 steps)
1. Get Azure AD Token
2. Get CMS Auth
3. Search Case
4. List Egress Files (gets latest by timestamp)
5. Validate Transfer
6. Initiate Transfer
7. Poll Status (loops until complete)
8. Verify Activity Log
9. Download Activity Log

## Response Validation
Each request validates:
- HTTP 200 status
- Required response fields
- Data structure

item.name: E2E: Netapp to Egress Copy

exec: // Per-run unique subfolder. Forward (Egress->NetApp) deposits the file
// here and the matching back-direction journey lists the same path; both
// compute the same suffix because it's derived from e2eRunId stamped once
// by 0. E2E Setup and propagated via the post-setup env. A second
// import-and-Run cycle in Postman Runner re-runs Setup, which stamps a
// fresh e2eRunId, so the new pass writes to a new sub-folder and does not
// FileExists-collide with leftovers from the prior pass.
// Deterministically pick the file Egress->NetApp Copy deposited this run.
// 'latest by lastModified' was unsafe -- it picked stale files from prior
// runs whose namesakes already exist in Egress 3. Unused - disclosed/,
console.log('[SELECTED] File deposited by Egress->NetApp Copy this run:');
console.log('  Path:', selectedFile.path, '| Size:', selectedFile.filesize, '| Modified:', selectedFile.lastModified);
// NetApp listing may not reflect the just-copied file yet. Fall back
// to a constructed path -- the Copy journey deposited the file at
// resolve it by path regardless of whether the listing has caught up.
console.warn('[FALLBACK] Expected file not in NetApp listing yet:', expectedName);
// Size unknown; the transfer validate call doesn't rely on it.
// Standalone `-TestsToRun netapp-to-egress`: no Copy journey ran,
// so egressCopyFileName isn't set. Fall back to the most-recently-
// modified file in the folder so this mode keeps working against
// whatever file the operator manually placed. The full E2E flow
// always takes the deterministic branch above.
const sortedFiles = validFiles.slice().sort((a, b) => new Date(b.lastModified) - new Date(a.lastModified));
console.warn('[FALLBACK] No egressCopyFileName in env; picking most-recent file:', fileName);
// Destination uniquification is now handled centrally by
// 10. Validate Transfer NetApp to Egress (prerequest), so it
// applies to both the full E2E path and this standalone path.
console.error('[ERROR] NetApp folder has no files to transfer.');
pm.test('NetApp folder has a file to transfer', function() {
pm.expect.fail('No valid files in NetApp Automation-Testing/');
pm.execution.setNextRequest('10. Validate Transfer NetApp to Egress');

item.description:
End-to-end test: Copy file from Egress to NetApp with activity log verification.

## Flow (9 steps)

1. Get Azure AD Token
    
2. Get CMS Auth
    
3. Search Case
    
4. List Egress Files (gets latest by timestamp)
    
5. Validate Transfer
    
6. Initiate Transfer
    
7. Poll Status (loops until complete)
    
8. Verify Activity Log
    
9. Download Activity Log
    

## Response Validation

Each request validates:

- HTTP 200 status
    
- Required response fields
    
- Data structure

exec: // Per-run unique subfolder. Forward (Egress->NetApp) deposits the file
// here and the matching back-direction journey lists the same path; both
// compute the same suffix because it's derived from e2eRunId stamped once
// by 0. E2E Setup and propagated via the post-setup env. A second
// import-and-Run cycle in Postman Runner re-runs Setup, which stamps a
// fresh e2eRunId, so the new pass writes to a new sub-folder and does not
// FileExists-collide with leftovers from the prior pass.
// Deterministically pick the file Egress->NetApp Move deposited this run.
// 'latest by lastModified' was unsafe -- it picked stale files from prior
// runs whose namesakes already exist in Egress 3. Unused - disclosed/,
console.log('[SELECTED] File deposited by Egress->NetApp Move this run:');
console.log('  Path:', selectedFile.path, '| Size:', selectedFile.filesize, '| Modified:', selectedFile.lastModified);
// Expected file is missing from the NetApp listing. In the full E2E
// flow this can't happen: the Move forward journey polled the transfer
// to Completed before this folder runs, so the file is in NetApp. The
// only way to reach this branch is a standalone `-TestsToRun
// netapp-move-to-egress` (or a Runner pass where the Move folder didn't
// run) -- the source was never moved here. Fail fast with a clear
// message instead of constructing a path to a file that doesn't exist
// and letting the transfer service produce a confusing downstream error.

'. Run the E2E: Egress to NetApp Move journey first (or use -TestsToRun all).');

// the stale netappFilePath from a previous folder (e.g. the Copy back
// 9.) would cause this folder to submit a transfer for the wrong file.
// Standalone `-TestsToRun netapp-to-egress`: no Copy journey ran,
// so egressMoveFileName isn't set. Fall back to the most-recently-
// modified file in the folder so this mode keeps working against
// whatever file the operator manually placed. The full E2E flow
// always takes the deterministic branch above.

// applies to both the full E2E path and this standalone path.

pm.test('NetApp folder has a file to transfer', function() {

## Flow (9 steps)

1. Get Azure AD Token
    
2. Get CMS Auth
    
3. Search Case

5. Validate Transfer

8. Verify Activity Log

- Required response fields
